The PR branch is already\nchecked out in the current working directory. The workflow has prepended `REPO`\nand `PR NUMBER` lines above this prompt — use them.\n\n## Your job\n\nAct as a deliberate counter-voice to the PR Review agent. Read the inline comments\nalready posted by the PR Review agent on this PR and push back selectively on those\nthat are wrong, over-eager, or low-signal. Nothing else.\n\n## What to push back on\n\nChallenge a comment when it meets one or more of these conditions:\n\n1. **Wrong fact** — the reviewer flagged something based on incorrect or outdated\n   information about a library API, language behavior, or external tool.\n2. **Overstated severity** — a `High` or `Critical` rating that should be `Medium`\n   or `Low` given the actual impact; or a `Medium` that is not a real issue.\n3. **Low-signal nit** — style preference, naming bikeshed, hypothetical future\n   requirement, or a \"have you considered…\" musing with no concrete problem stated.\n4. **False positive** — the surrounding context (PR intent, adjacent code, tests,\n   existing conventions in the repo) shows the flagged code is correct and\n   intentional.\n\nDo **not** push back on:\n- Clear correctness bugs, even minor ones.\n- Security issues with a concrete and plausible attack path.\n- Performance concerns that are measurably significant in the actual usage pattern.\n\nWhen in doubt, stay silent. Only push back when you have a concrete reason.\n\n## How to gather context\n\n- `gh pr view ${PR_NUMBER} --json title,body,state,additions,deletions,changedFiles,headRefName` — PR metadata.\n- `gh pr diff ${PR_NUMBER}` — the full diff; primary input.\n- `gh api repos/${REPO}/pulls/${PR_NUMBER}/comments` — all inline review comments.\n  Filter for those posted by `github-actions[bot]` whose body contains `**Severity:**` —\n  those are the PR Review agent's comments. Note each comment's `id` field.\n- Read files in the working directory to understand context around flagged lines.\n\n### Verifying external facts\n\nDo not rely on training-data recall for anything externally verifiable.\n\n- **Library / framework / SDK / API / CLI questions** — use Context7\n  (`mcp__context7__resolve-library-id` then `mcp__context7__query-docs`).\n- **Everything else** (model versions, CVEs, changelogs) — `WebSearch`, then\n  `WebFetch` for specific pages. When fetching package docs, prefer the GitHub\n  source over npmjs.com — npmjs.com commonly returns 403.\n\nIf verification confirms the reviewer's claim, leave the comment alone.\n\n## How to post pushback\n\nReply directly to a specific inline comment via:\n\n```sh\ngh api \"repos/${REPO}/pulls/comments/${COMMENT_ID}/replies\" -f body=\"...\"\n```\n\nEach reply must be concise:\n- One clear sentence stating why the comment is wrong, over-eager, or low-signal.\n- One sentence of concrete evidence: a code reference, a verified API doc, or a\n  repo convention that directly supports your pushback.\n\nDo **not** post a top-level \"LGTM\" or blanket response. If every review comment\nis valid, post nothing and stop.\n\n## When you're done\n\nStop immediately after your last tool call. Do not list your findings, do not\nrecap which replies you posted. The runner will exit.\n"}

Reads inline comments posted by the\nPR Review agent and pushes back selectively on those that are wrong, over-eager,\nor low-signal. Reply-only — never approves, merges, or pushes code.\n\nPart of the agentic workflow sketched in [`docs/agentic-workflow.md`](../../docs/agentic-workflow.md).\n\n## Files\n\n- `prompt.md` — the agent's prompt. Edit this to change behavior.\n- `../../.github/workflows/pr-refinement.yml` — workflow that runs the agent.\n\n## How it runs\n\nThe workflow `pr-refinement.yml` runs on a daily cron schedule. A `discover` job\nfinds all open PRs that:\n\n1. Already have at least one inline comment from the PR Review agent (identified\n   by `github-actions[bot]` authorship and the `**Severity:**` marker in the body).\n2. Have not yet reached the configured round cap (`MAX_REFINEMENT_ROUNDS`,\n   defaulting to `1`).\n\nFor each eligible PR, a matrix `refine` job checks out the PR head, prepends repo\nand PR number to `prompt.md`, and feeds the result to `anthropics/claude-code-action@v1`.\n\n**Model:** `claude-opus-4-8` — a different model than PR Review's default\n(the action's built-in model, no `--model` override) to hedge against\nsingle-model review bias. See [Design choices](#design-choices).\n\n## Round cap (stop condition)\n\nAfter each refinement pass the workflow adds or updates a label\n`refinement-rounds:N` on the PR, where `N` is the total completed rounds. On\nthe next daily run, PRs where `N ≥ MAX_REFINEMENT_ROUNDS` are skipped.\n\n`MAX_REFINEMENT_ROUNDS` defaults to `1`. Override by setting a repository\nvariable of the same name (**Settings → Variables → Actions**).\n\n## Manual run\n\n```sh\ngh workflow run pr-refinement.yml\n```\n\nRuns against all currently eligible open PRs.\n\n## Editing the prompt\n\nEdit `prompt.md` directly. The workflow reads it at run-time, so changes take\neffect on the next run without any other plumbing.\n\n## Design choices\n\n- **Different model than PR Review.** PR Review uses `claude-code-action@v1`\n  with no `--model` override. PR Refinement pins `claude-opus-4-8` so the\n  counter-voice comes from a genuinely independent model — a named design\n  choice in `docs/agentic-workflow.md` (\"Review ↔ Refinement as an adversarial\n  pair\").\n- **Cron, not event-triggered.** Refinement runs once daily rather than firing\n  on every new review comment, giving reviewer comments time to settle before\n  the counter-pass.\n- **Selective pushback only.** The agent replies only to comments it can\n  concretely refute; it posts no summaries, blanket LGTMs, or top-level\n  comments.\n- **Label-based round tracking.** `refinement-rounds:N` labels are visible\n  directly in the PR UI, making the stop condition auditable without querying\n  the API.\n\n## What's deliberately not configured\n\n- No `--max-turns` — runs to completion.\n- No `--system-prompt` flag — `prompt.md` *is* the system-level guidance.\n- `track_progress: false` — no sticky tracking comment clutters the PR thread.\n\n## Required setup (one-time)\n\nSame credentials as PR Review — see [`agents/pr-review/README.md`](../pr-review/README.md)\nfor full details.\n\n- `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN` repository secret (Claude Pro/Max OAuth) **or**\n  `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` for per-token billing.\n- `CONTEXT7_API_KEY` repository secret for library doc lookups.\n"}

1	name: Junior Developer agent

4	  workflow_dispatch:
5	    inputs:

7	        description: 'Jira ticket key (e.g. KAN-42)'

12	        required: false

21	      contents: write

23	      id-token: write

31	          # when the PAT isn't configured (workflow-file changes will then

39	      - name: Install jira CLI

41	
42	      - name: Put bin/ on PATH

55	          : "${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY:?JIRA_PROJECT_KEY repo variable is required}"

name: Junior Developer agent

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      ticket_key:
        description: 'Jira ticket key (e.g. KAN-42)'
        required: true
        type: string
      max_turns:
        description: 'Override --max-turns (default 5). Use a small value to exercise the stop condition.'
        required: false
        type: string
        default: '5'

jobs:
  implement:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 90
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
      id-token: write
      actions: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          # PAT with workflows:write so the agent can push branches that
          # add or modify .github/workflows/*. Falls back to GITHUB_TOKEN
          # when the PAT isn't configured (workflow-file changes will then
          # be rejected at push time, by design).
          token: ${{ secrets.DEV_PAT || secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'

      - name: Install jira CLI
        run: bash scripts/install-jira-cli.sh

      - name: Put bin/ on PATH
        run: echo "$PWD/bin" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"

      - name: Write jira config
        env:
          JIRA_SERVER: ${{ vars.JIRA_SERVER }}
          JIRA_LOGIN: ${{ vars.JIRA_LOGIN }}
          JIRA_PROJECT_KEY: ${{ vars.JIRA_PROJECT_KEY }}
          JIRA_BOARD_ID: ${{ vars.JIRA_BOARD_ID }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          : "${JIRA_SERVER:?JIRA_SERVER repo variable is required}"
          : "${JIRA_LOGIN:?JIRA_LOGIN repo variable is required}"
          : "${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY:?JIRA_PROJECT_KEY repo variable is required}"
          mkdir -p .jira
          cat > .jira/config.yml <<EOF
          installation: Cloud
          server: ${JIRA_SERVER}
          login: ${JIRA_LOGIN}
          project:
              key: ${JIRA_PROJECT_KEY}
              type: classic
          board:
              id: ${JIRA_BOARD_ID:-0}
          EOF

      - name: Install Claude Code CLI
        run: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code

      - name: Configure git user
        run: |
          git config --global user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config --global user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"

      - name: Create feature branch
        env:
          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          git fetch --depth=1 origin main
          git checkout -B "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" origin/main
          # Reset the remote ref so re-runs of the same ticket start
          # clean — matches the local `checkout -B` semantic. A prior
          # failed run can leave commits on the remote that diverge
          # from current main, which would block fast-forward push.
          git push --force origin "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}"

      - id: prompt
        env:
          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
        run: |
          {
            echo 'PROMPT<<PROMPT_EOF'
            echo "TICKET_KEY: ${TICKET_KEY}"
            echo "DATE: $(date -u +%F)"
            echo
            cat agents/junior-dev/prompt.md
            echo PROMPT_EOF
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - id: claude
        name: Run Claude (headless)
        env:
          CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
          CONTEXT7_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.CONTEXT7_API_KEY }}
          JIRA_CONFIG_FILE: ${{ github.workspace }}/.jira/config.yml
          JIRA_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.JIRA_API_TOKEN }}
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.DEV_PAT || secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          PROMPT: ${{ steps.prompt.outputs.PROMPT }}
          MAX_TURNS: ${{ inputs.max_turns }}
        run: |
          set +e
          claude -p "$PROMPT" \
            --permission-mode acceptEdits \
            --max-turns "$MAX_TURNS" \
            --output-format stream-json \
            --verbose \
            --allowedTools "Bash(jira issue view:*),Bash(bin/jira issue view:*),Bash(git add:*),Bash(git commit:*),Bash(git status:*),Bash(git diff:*),Bash(git log:*),Bash(git push:*),Bash(git rev-parse:*),Bash(git remote:*),Bash(git config --get:*),Bash(git config --list),Bash(git branch:*),Bash(git show:*),Bash(gh pr create:*),Bash(gh pr view:*),Bash(gh auth status),Bash(gh api repos/:*),Bash(npm test:*),Bash(npm run:*),Bash(ls:*),Bash(cat:*),Bash(printenv:*),Bash(env),Read,Glob,Grep,Edit,Write,mcp__context7__resolve-library-id,mcp__context7__query-docs,WebSearch,WebFetch" \
            > stream.jsonl
          rc=$?
          echo "claude_exit=$rc" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          # Always succeed; downstream steps handle non-zero claude exit.
          exit 0

      - name: Convert log to JSON array
        if: always()
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          if [ -s stream.jsonl ]; then
            jq -s '.' stream.jsonl > execution.json
          else
            echo '[]' > execution.json
          fi

      - name: Open fallback Draft PR if agent didn't
        if: always()
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.DEV_PAT || secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          TICKET_KEY: ${{ inputs.ticket_key }}
          CLAUDE_EXIT: ${{ steps.claude.outputs.claude_exit }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail

          # Skip if the agent already opened a PR for this branch.
          if gh pr view "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" --json url >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            echo "PR already exists for junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}; skipping fallback."
            exit 0
          fi

          # Need at least one commit beyond origin/main to have something to push.
          commits=$(git rev-list --count "origin/main..HEAD" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
          if [ "$commits" -eq 0 ]; then
            echo "No commits on branch; nothing to push."
            exit 0
          fi

          # Push the branch (no-op if the agent already pushed).
          git push -u origin "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" || true

          gh pr create --draft \
            --base main \
            --head "junior-dev/${TICKET_KEY}" \
            --title "[WIP] [${TICKET_KEY}] junior-dev exited before opening PR" \
            --body "Junior-dev agent run terminated before opening a PR itself (claude exit code ${CLAUDE_EXIT:-unknown}; likely \`--max-turns\` cap or runtime error). Branch holds whatever the agent committed. See \`.github/agent-logs/junior-dev/\` for the run log."

      - name: Commit execution log to main
        if: always() && hashFiles('execution.json') != ''
        env:
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
        run: |
          set -e
          git fetch --depth=1 origin main
          git checkout -f -B main origin/main
          DATE="$(date -u +%F)"
          DEST=".github/agent-logs/junior-dev/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json"
          mkdir -p "$(dirname "$DEST")"
          cp execution.json "$DEST"
          git add "$DEST"
          git commit -m "agent log: junior-dev run ${RUN_ID}"
          git push origin main

      - name: Trigger agent improver
        if: always() && hashFiles('execution.json') != ''
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          RUN_ID: ${{ github.run_id }}
        run: |
          DATE="$(date -u +%F)"
          gh workflow run agent-improver.yml \
            -f agent_name=junior-dev \
            -f log_path=".github/agent-logs/junior-dev/${DATE}/${RUN_ID}.json" \
            -f log_ref=main
